concepts.title: Managerial Functions
concepts.description: Henri Fayol identified five functions of management: planning, organizing, commanding, coordinating, and controlling.

concepts.title: Principles of Management
concepts.description: Principles of management refer to the fundamental concepts that guide the actions of managers in organizations.

concepts.title: Scientific Management
concepts.description: Scientific management, also known as Taylorism, emphasizes the use of scientific methods to improve efficiency and productivity in organizations.

concepts.title: Management Functions Mnemonic
concepts.description: Use the 'POCCC' mnemonic to remember the managerial functions: Planning, Organizing, Coordinating, Controlling, and Commanding.

concepts.title: Confusing Management with Administration
concepts.description: Management is a broader concept that involves planning, organizing, and controlling, whereas administration refers to the day-to-day activities of an organization.

concepts.title: Fayol's 14 Principles of Management
concepts.description: Fayol's 14 principles of management are divided into two categories: tactical (6 principles) and strategic (8 principles).

concepts.title: Key Features of Bureaucracy
concepts.description: Bureaucracy is characterized by a hierarchical structure, division of labor, and a strong emphasis on rules and procedures.

concepts.title: Importance of Communication in Management
concepts.description: Effective communication is essential for successful management, as it helps to build trust, motivate employees, and achieve organizational goals.
